what_fails: PowerShell 5.1 treats << as a redirection operator — heredoc syntax is bash-only
what_works: Write message to a temp file then: git commit -F tempfile; OR use PowerShell here-string written to file first

what_fails: PowerShell 5.1: && is not a valid statement separator — parse error
what_works: Use semicolons with conditional: A; if ($LASTEXITCODE -eq 0) { B }  OR upgrade to pwsh 7+ where && works natively

what_fails: Wraps each stderr line in an ErrorRecord object; sets $? to $false even when exit code is 0; corrupts error stream
what_works: Do not redirect stderr; PowerShell captures it separately. Check exit code via $LASTEXITCODE, not $?. Use $? only for cmdlets, not native exes

what_fails: Interactive flag hangs in non-interactive shell sessions (Cursor terminal, Claude Code, CI)
what_works: git rebase --autosquash instead of rebase -i; git add <specific-files> instead of add -i; git commit --fixup + rebase for squash workflows

command_pattern: bash <<'EOF' ... EOF inside PowerShell call to native git
what_fails: PowerShell does not parse bash heredoc syntax — the <<'EOF' is treated as a redirection, not a here-doc
what_works: Use bash -c '...' for bash heredocs on Windows WSL; OR use PowerShell here-string @'...\n'@ written to a temp file and passed to git via -F

command_pattern: cd path && command in a single Shell tool call
what_fails: PowerShell 5.1: && not valid. Even if using semicolons, cd does not persist across tool calls if the shell is stateless
what_works: Use the working_directory parameter of the Shell tool to set CWD per-call. Or chain with semicolons: cd path; command (state persists within one call)

what_fails: Scripts that use import.meta.url or __dirname to compute paths will break if run from wrong CWD — resolved path goes to caller's CWD, not script's directory
what_works: Always set working_directory to the repo root where package.json lives, OR use npm run <script> which sets CWD correctly. Verify with: node -e "console.log(process.cwd())"

command_pattern: pip install -e . in Windows with spaces in path
what_fails: Paths with spaces (e.g. C:\Users\Administrator\Documents\LianaBanyanPlatform) may cause install errors if the path is not quoted in the shell invocation
what_works: Always quote paths: pip install -e "C:\path with spaces\pkg"; use Set-Location first then pip install -e . from that directory

command_pattern: git tag -a v1.0.0 -m "message" with multi-line message in PowerShell
what_fails: Multi-line -m strings in PowerShell require special quoting; embedded newlines via `n are sometimes not passed correctly to git
what_works: Use single-line -m message for tags; for annotated tags needing multi-line, write message to file and use: git tag -a v1.0.0 -F tagmsg.txt

command_pattern: Get-Content file | ForEach-Object vs cat file in PowerShell
what_fails: cat is an alias for Get-Content in PowerShell — it works but outputs objects not raw strings; piping to grep (which doesn't exist) fails. Use Select-String instead of grep
what_works: Get-Content file | Select-String pattern  (PowerShell native grep). For raw text pass-through: Get-Content file -Raw. Avoid cat + grep idioms from bash
